summary: Full backend + frontend testing of the per-member Time Plan refactor. Backend (pytest, 14/14 passing): member palette colour is a 7-char hex, distinct across consecutive members (Alice=#60A5FA, Theresa=#F472B6, Carol=#34D399), GET back-fills + persists, PUT color override persists. Events: non-admin's GET is forced to their own owner_member_id even when ?user_id=<admin>; admin GET no filter returns ALL family owners; admin GET with ?user_ids=a,b restricts to those. POST as non-admin defaults owner to self; POST as non-admin with user_id=<other> -> 403 'Only family admins can create events for someone else'; POST as admin with user_id=<member> -> 200 with owner_member_id == that member. PUT by other non-admin -> 403; PUT by admin can reassign user_id/owner_member_id. DELETE by owner OK, by other non-admin 403, by admin OK. Family B admin cannot list, edit, or delete Family A events (multi-tenant 403/404). Frontend (Playwright, 1920x1080): registered family, bootstrapped Alice as admin, logged in via localStorage seeding; /time-plan shows the calendar with Alice's pill highlighted and the open-family-view-btn visible. Adding Theresa via /api/family/members and reloading made her pill appear automatically in the user-switcher; the coloured dot inside each pill matches the API color (Alice rgb(96,165,250), Theresa rgb(244,114,182)). Family View dialog opens, the toggle for Theresa adds her to the visible set and the badge becomes 2; family-view-all and family-view-reset both work. Admin opened EventDialog, switched the For-select to Theresa, saved -> API confirms event 'TEST_TheresaEvent' has owner_member_id == Theresa, and the calendar renders it in Theresa's pink. After swapping member_token to Theresa (non-admin), open-family-view-btn count is 0 (hidden), Alice pill is disabled (greyed) and Theresa's pill is active, EventDialog owner select trigger is disabled. Switching mfml_lang to ar and de re-renders the page with zero raw 'tp.familyView/viewingMember/viewingFamily/cantEditOthers' key leaks (German screenshot shows 'Kalender von Theresa' subtitle).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 2802 lines — events routes are still inline. The earlier guidance to split server.py into routers (events, kids_money, kids_money_goals, etc.) remains; events alone with helpers _event_owner_id / _normalize_owner_filter / _ensure_event_writable would be ~150 lines in /app/backend/routers/events.py.
TimePlan.jsx is 679 lines — close to the 700-line guideline. Sub-components like FamilyViewDialog, UserPillSwitcher and CalendarGrid could be extracted.

EventCreate.color is required (Pydantic 422 when omitted). The frontend always sends a colour, but worth noting in API docs because /api/family/members generates a colour automatically while /api/events still demands one from the client.

_normalize_owner_filter silently drops the user_id/user_ids filter for non-admins instead of returning 403. Current behaviour matches the spec (members see only their own calendar by default) but a 'why didn't my filter apply?' UI hint could be useful.
Auth response for /auth/member/select returns is_family_admin only on the inner member payload, not as a top-level field — minor; UI relies on the cached member object which works.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (8/8 flows: calendar load + active pill, second member auto-appears, coloured dots, family-view dialog + toggle + badge, only-me/show-everyone, admin creates event for Theresa with her colour, non-admin disabled pills + hidden family-view + disabled owner select, AR+DE translations no key leaks)

test_credentials: Created fresh per run: qa-tp-<ts>@example.com / Pass1234! ; bootstrap admin Alice PIN 1234, Theresa PIN 2222, Carol PIN 3333. Member tokens via /api/auth/member/select.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run registers two fresh families (TEST_TP_Family) via /api/auth/register; Family A has admin Alice + non-admin Bob + non-admin Carol; Family B has its own admin for isolation tests. The Playwright run separately registers TEST_TP_UI with admin Alice + Theresa.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_time_plan.py -v (export $(grep -v ^# /app/frontend/.env | xargs) first to pick up REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). Key data-testids: open-family-view-btn, family-view-dialog, family-view-toggle-{member_id}, family-view-reset, family-view-all, user-pill-{member_id}, user-switcher, add-event-btn, event-dialog, event-user-select, event-save-btn, day-popover-{iso}, edit-event-{id}, delete-event-{id}. To drive UI as admin or non-admin, register via /api/auth/register, add members through the admin's member token, then write mfml_account_token, mfml_member_token, mfml_account, mfml_family, mfml_member, mfml_auth_ok=true, mfml_lang to localStorage and visit /time-plan. EventCreate POST body MUST include 'color' (Pydantic required field).
rca of the issue: N/A — all expected behaviours verified; no bugs found. Note the one transient test-script issue: EventCreate requires 'color', initial helper omitted it (422) — fixed by passing color='#60A5FA' in _create_event.
